codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of variable font axis rendering across 9 files (+939 lines). The feature correctly adds: (1) AxisValue struct and axis normalization in shaping/rasterize.rs, (2) rasterize_with_axes() with gvar delta interpolation and phantom point advance adjustment, (3) shape_with_variations() wrapping HarfRust Variation/ShaperInstance, (4) LRU cache key extended to (glyph_id, font_size, axis_hash), (5) TextRun.axis_hash field for scene graph flow, (6) axis_values_hash() FNV-1a hash for deterministic cache keys. All 6 expected behaviors are tested with 11 new tests. All validation contract assertions (VAL-VARFONT-002, VAL-VARFONT-003, VAL-CACHE-005, VAL-CROSS-002) are covered. Out-of-range clamping is handled correctly. Backward compatibility preserved via default axis_hash=0 delegation in get/insert methods. All existing TextRun construction sites updated.

issues.description: Code duplication: rasterize_with_axes() duplicates ~120 lines of the rendering pipeline (scaling, oversampling, segment flattening, rasterization, downsampling, FIR filter, stem darkening) from rasterize(). The only difference is how the outline is extracted. A shared inner function accepting the outline extraction result would halve the code. Non-blocking per 'review the output, not the code' principle — tests confirm correctness.

Potential i16 truncation in bounding box recomputation: after applying gvar deltas, 'outline.x_min = x_min as i16' truncates i32 to i16 without checking. In practice, font-unit coordinates with gvar deltas stay well within i16 range for standard fonts, but the silent truncation could cause rendering artifacts for extreme axis values on unusual fonts.

Inaccurate SAFETY comment: 'TextRun is #[repr(C)] with no padding' is incorrect after adding axis_hash: u32. The struct now has 2 bytes of internal padding between font_size (offset 20, u16) and axis_hash (offset 24, u32). Total size is 28 bytes, not 26. Serialization still works correctly because both writer and reader use the same struct definition and size_of::<TextRun>(). The comment should be corrected to 'TextRun is #[repr(C)]' without the 'no padding' claim. Note: this inaccuracy pre-existed — the struct had 2 bytes of tail padding before axis_hash was added (size was 24, not 22).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker's skillFeedback suggests marking visual QEMU testing conditional for library-only features that don't affect the current rendering path. The font-rendering-worker skill mandates QEMU visual verification for 'features that affect the display pipeline' but axis rendering is infrastructure that isn't wired into the compositor's active rendering path yet. The skill could clarify when visual testing is required vs optional.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The font-rendering-worker skill's visual testing step could be marked conditional for library-only features that don't affect the display pipeline's current rendering path'. Worker correctly skipped QEMU testing since all axis_hash values are 0 in the current compositor.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The SAFETY comment on push_text_runs (scene/lib.rs:540) claims 'TextRun is #[repr(C)] with no padding' but the struct has internal padding. This inaccuracy pre-dated this feature (the struct had tail padding before axis_hash was added). AGENTS.md doesn't have guidance on verifying padding claims in SAFETY comments when modifying repr(C) structs used for cross-process shared memory serialization.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/libraries/scene/lib.rs:540 — comment says 'no padding' but TextRun is 28 bytes with 2 bytes padding between font_size (offset 20) and axis_hash (offset 24). Verified via rustc layout check: TextRun size=28, align=4.

summary: PASS. Variable font axis rendering is correctly implemented with comprehensive test coverage for all 4 validation contract assertions. The implementation adds axis-aware rasterization (gvar interpolation), shaping (HarfRust Variation/ShaperInstance), cache key extension (3-tuple with axis_hash), and scene graph flow (TextRun.axis_hash field). All 11 new tests verify the expected behaviors: different coverage at different weights, interpolation at intermediate values (>5% difference), out-of-range clamping without panic, and axis-keyed cache separation. Three non-blocking issues noted: code duplication in rasterize_with_axes, potential i16 truncation in bounding box recomputation, and an inaccurate SAFETY comment about struct padding. All 1105+ tests pass, clean bare-metal build.
